hyperedges.label: Social/community platform icon group (Bluesky, Discord, X)

nodes.label: graphify Skill Trigger Rule
nodes.rationale: Instructs Claude to load the installed graphify skill/instructions before doing anything else whenever the user types /graphify, so the slash command always resolves to the packaged SKILL.md rather than ad-hoc behavior.

nodes.label: --mode deep
nodes.rationale: Requests thorough extraction with aggressive INFERRED edges (indirect deps, shared assumptions, latent couplings); uncertain ones are marked AMBIGUOUS instead of omitted, trading recall for a wider audit surface.

nodes.label: AST structural extraction (Part A)
nodes.rationale: Deterministic and free (no LLM, no API key) - a code-only corpus can skip semantic extraction entirely and rely on AST alone, run in parallel with semantic subagents to save 5-15s on large corpora.

nodes.label: Semantic extraction (Part B)
nodes.rationale: Only needed for docs/papers/images. Uses Gemini if GEMINI_API_KEY/GOOGLE_API_KEY is set, otherwise falls back to the host agent itself as the LLM via dispatched subagents - graphify never reads ANTHROPIC_API_KEY or other provider keys and must never block on a missing one.

nodes.label: Semantic extraction cache (Step B0)
nodes.rationale: Cache entries are keyed to the extraction-spec prompt file (SPEC_PATH) so that when the prompt changes (a graphify upgrade), stale entries are re-extracted instead of replayed, while unchanged prompts keep their cached entries (#1939).

nodes.label: Shrink-guard (#479)
nodes.rationale: to_json() refuses to write graph.json when the new graph is smaller than the existing one, so a bad/partial extraction can never silently clobber a good graph.json, GRAPH_REPORT.md, or analysis sidecar.

nodes.label: Graph health check (Step 4.5)
nodes.rationale: Read-only, non-aborting diagnostic that surfaces edge collapse, dangling/missing endpoints, and self-loops - the silent-corruption modes of incremental updates and AST/LLM id mismatches - so integrity issues stay visible without blocking the pipeline.

nodes.label: Debounce (default 3s)
nodes.rationale: Waits until file activity stops before triggering a rebuild, so a wave of parallel agent writes doesn't cause a rebuild per individual file.

nodes.label: --neo4j / --neo4j-push export
nodes.rationale: Uses Cypher MERGE so pushing to a live Neo4j instance is safe to re-run without creating duplicate nodes/edges.

nodes.label: --falkordb / --falkordb-push export
nodes.rationale: FalkorDB's GRAPH.QUERY runs one statement at a time (no bulk cypher-shell-style import like Neo4j), so the plain cypher.txt export is only useful as a portable artifact - --falkordb-push is preferred to actually load a graph, also via idempotent MERGE.

nodes.label: confidence_score rubric
nodes.rationale: Uses a discrete set of five values (0.95/0.85/0.75/0.65/0.55) instead of a continuous range because production data showed the continuous guidance collapsing to a bimodal distribution (>50% at 0.5, >40% at 0.85+); models follow discrete rubrics more faithfully.

nodes.label: Node ID format rule
nodes.rationale: IDs must use the full repo-relative path (every directory level, not just the immediate parent) so semantic-extraction IDs match the AST extractor's IDs exactly; using just the filename or immediate parent creates orphan ghost-duplicate nodes, requiring `graphify extract --force` to rebuild cleanly under the old format.

nodes.label: source_file verbatim rule
nodes.rationale: source_file must be copied character-for-character from FILE_LIST (no basename shortening, no re-relativizing) so the full build and incremental --update share the same base path, letting build_merge's replace-on-re-extract match the existing node instead of accumulating a duplicate.

nodes.label: Monorepo/multi-service merge flow
nodes.rationale: The skill pipeline always writes graphify-out/ to the current working directory, so running the skill separately on each subfolder of a monorepo would clobber the same output dir; the CLI must be invoked directly per subfolder (graphify extract) so each writes graphify-out/ inside its own scanned path, then merge-graphs combines them.

nodes.label: git post-commit hook (graphify hook install)
nodes.rationale: Triggers once per commit (via git diff HEAD~1) rather than running a background process; re-runs AST extraction only on changed code files and rebuilds graph.json/GRAPH_REPORT.md with no LLM cost. Doc/image changes are ignored by the hook and require a manual /graphify --update.

nodes.label: Constrained query expansion (Step 0)
nodes.rationale: The graphify query CLI matches nodes via case-folded substring + IDF with no stemming, synonyms, or cross-language matching, so a wording mismatch between the user's question and the graph's node-label vocabulary collapses the answer to noise; the fix is to expand the query against the graph's own extracted vocabulary first, picking only tokens that already exist in it rather than inventing near-synonyms.

nodes.label: Work memory / self-improving loop (--outcome)
nodes.rationale: Tagging save-result with --outcome useful/dead_end/corrected lets future sessions learn: useful answers become preferred sources, dead ends are skipped instead of re-derived, and corrections record the right answer - closing a feedback loop across sessions.

nodes.label: Whisper initial prompt (domain hint)
nodes.rationale: The host model composes a one-sentence domain hint itself from existing god-node labels (or a generic fallback if the corpus is video-only) instead of making a separate API call, biasing Whisper's transcription vocabulary toward the corpus domain at no extra cost.

nodes.label: Code-only update shortcut
nodes.rationale: When every changed file in an --update run is a code file, semantic extraction (subagents) is skipped entirely and only AST re-extraction runs, since code-only changes need no LLM.

nodes.label: build_merge()
nodes.rationale: Reads graph.json directly without a NetworkX round-trip so edge direction (calls/implements/imports) is always preserved (#801); every source_file present in the new extraction is dropped from the base graph before merging, so re-extracted (changed) files replace their stale nodes instead of being deduplicated after the fact (#1344), while prune_sources is reserved strictly for genuinely deleted files.

nodes.label: graphify project rules (native CLAUDE.md section)
nodes.rationale: Directs codebase questions to graphify query/path/explain first because they return a scoped subgraph usually much smaller than GRAPH_REPORT.md or raw grep output, and directs code changes to trigger `graphify update .` (AST-only, no API cost) to keep the graph current.
